methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $106M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Until, a Series A biotech company founded by Laura Deming and Hunter Davis, has raised $106M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using total raised ($106M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next stage yields an implied valuation of $212M. This method is defensible given Until's capital intensity and stage positioning. Peer-median anchoring is not applicable—while 8 same-sector private comps exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier companies yielded insufficient datapoints (n<3). Public-comp P/S anchoring also cannot be applied because Until's revenue is not on file, which is typical for early-stage private companies. The public Biotech & Health median P/S of 6.9x across 3 tickers establishes sector context but lacks a target revenue input for direct application.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $180M to $244M, with a median of $212M. This range is constrained by reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include Until's underlying revenue trajectory, cash burn profile, and time to next funding event—factors that would sharpen the range if available. The valuation remains sensitive to the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier assumption for this particular company and stage.
